title: Multi-K averaging methodology achieves 3x variance reduction

content.discovery: Single-K diagnostics (K=6 only) achieve CV≈14.8%, missing binding effects that are revealed at higher statistical power. Multi-K averaging across K=[4,6,8,10] achieves CV≈5.1%, a 3x variance reduction.
content.impact: All future diagnostics must use multi-K protocol. Single-K results are unreliable for effect size estimation.
content.example_revision: Task #14 (clip bounds diagnostic) used K=6 only and concluded clip bounds are non-binding (NARROW_TIGHT d=0.610, p=0.096). Multi-K reanalysis shows VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3] at d=7.583, p=0.000 — binding effect confirmed.
content.implication: Many Phase 1 diagnostics may need revalidation with multi-K protocol to avoid false negatives.
